features: F1: Project Scaffolding — full app/ directory structure, start.sh/start.bat scripts, Python venv setup, Bun frontend setup, Vite proxy config for /api, and .env loading from absolute path
F2: Database Schema & Repository Layer — all four SQLite tables defined in schema.py, async repository.py with full CRUD for conversations, messages, videos, and chunks; no raw SQL in route handlers
F3: Video Seed Data — seed.py with 10 mock AI/coding YouTube videos (300–600 word transcripts each), auto-runs on first startup via FastAPI lifespan hook when videos table is empty
F4: Docling Chunking Integration — chunker.py wraps HybridChunker(max_tokens=512, merge_peers=True), accepts a video dict, builds a DoclingDocument, and returns a list of contextualized text strings

criteria.name: directory_structure_complete
criteria.description: All of the following directories exist: app/frontend/src/components/, app/frontend/src/hooks/, app/frontend/src/lib/, app/backend/db/, app/backend/rag/, app/backend/llm/, app/backend/routes/, app/backend/data/. All of the following files exist at their specified paths: start.sh, start.bat, app/backend/main.py, app/backend/config.py, app/backend/db/schema.py, app/backend/db/repository.py, app/backend/data/seed.py, app/backend/rag/chunker.py, app/frontend/vite.config.ts, app/frontend/package.json, app/frontend/tsconfig.json. No directory or file from this list may be absent.

criteria.name: single_command_startup
criteria.description: Running start.bat (Windows) or start.sh (Unix) successfully installs dependencies, creates the Python venv if missing, runs 'bun install' if node_modules is missing, starts FastAPI on port 8000, and starts the Vite dev server on port 5173 — all without manual intervention. If port 8000 or 5173 is already occupied, the script must print a human-readable error message identifying the conflicting port and exit with a non-zero code rather than silently hanging or producing a cryptic OS error.

criteria.name: vite_api_proxy_configured
criteria.description: vite.config.ts contains a proxy rule that forwards all requests matching /api to http://localhost:8000, verified by the presence of the proxy configuration block. Confirmed at runtime: a GET to http://localhost:5173/api/health returns an HTTP response (any 2xx or 404 from FastAPI is acceptable; a connection-refused or network error is not).

criteria.name: env_loading_from_absolute_path
criteria.description: config.py uses python-dotenv's load_dotenv() with an absolute path to the project-root .env file. The path must be computed dynamically at runtime using Path(__file__).resolve() and .parents[] traversal — it must NOT be a hardcoded string literal containing a specific username or machine path. After loading, the OPENROUTER_API_KEY variable is accessible as a config constant. If OPENROUTER_API_KEY is absent or empty in the .env file, config.py logs a warning to stderr but does not raise an exception at import time, allowing the rest of the application to start.

criteria.name: all_four_db_tables_created
criteria.description: On backend startup, schema.py creates all four tables — videos, chunks, conversations, messages — with the exact columns, types, and constraints specified (UUIDs as TEXT primary keys, FK relationships, TIMESTAMP fields). Running the app from a clean state (no pre-existing chat.db) produces a valid chat.db at app/backend/data/chat.db. Verified by querying sqlite_master and confirming all four table names and their column definitions are present.

criteria.name: repository_pattern_enforced
criteria.description: repository.py exposes async methods covering all required CRUD operations: create/get/list/delete for conversations; create/list for messages; create/list for videos; create/list for chunks. No route handler file under app/backend/routes/ contains any raw SQL string (i.e., no file in that directory contains the tokens SELECT, INSERT, UPDATE, or DELETE as SQL literals). All DB access is delegated through repository methods.

criteria.name: seed_runs_on_empty_db_only
criteria.description: On first launch with no existing database, seed.py is invoked automatically via the FastAPI lifespan startup hook and inserts exactly 10 video records. On subsequent launches with data already present, the seed script does NOT re-insert videos (idempotency check: video count remains exactly 10 after two consecutive startups, verified by querying SELECT COUNT(*) FROM videos after each startup).

criteria.name: seed_data_content_quality
criteria.description: Each of the 10 seeded videos has: a non-empty title matching one of the 10 topics listed in the spec; a non-empty description; a YouTube URL matching the format https://www.youtube.com/watch?v= followed by exactly 11 alphanumeric or hyphen/underscore characters; and a transcript of at least 300 words. Topic-keyword spot-checks: the RAG-themed video's transcript must contain the words 'chunking' and 'embedding'; the vector-database-themed video's transcript must contain 'FAISS' or 'Pinecone'.

criteria.name: seed_progress_logged
criteria.description: During seeding, stdout contains log lines indicating progress — at minimum one log message per video inserted (e.g., 'Seeding video 1/10: Building AI Agents from Scratch') and a final summary line confirming completion (e.g., 'Seeding complete: 10 videos inserted'). If seeding is skipped because data already exists, a single log line must confirm the skip (e.g., 'Videos table already populated, skipping seed').

criteria.name: chunker_returns_contextualized_strings
criteria.description: chunker.py's main function, when called with a valid video dict containing a multi-paragraph transcript (at least 3 paragraphs), returns a Python list of at least 2 non-empty strings. Each string must contain substantive paragraph content (not just whitespace or punctuation). Where HybridChunker's contextualize method prepends heading breadcrumbs, those breadcrumbs must appear in the returned strings. When called with a video dict whose transcript field is an empty string or None, the function must return an empty list rather than raising an unhandled exception.

criteria.name: chunker_respects_max_tokens
criteria.description: HybridChunker is initialized with max_tokens=512 and merge_peers=True. No individual chunk returned by chunker.py exceeds 512 tokens when tokenized with a standard tokenizer. As a proxy check (verifiable without a tokenizer): no returned chunk string exceeds 2500 characters in length.

criteria.name: repository_uuid_generation
criteria.description: All records inserted via repository.py receive a valid UUID4 as their primary key id. Verified by inspecting chat.db after seeding: all id columns in all four tables contain strings matching the UUID4 format (8-4-4-4-12 lowercase hexadecimal pattern, e.g., xxxxxxxx-xxxx-4xxx-yxxx-xxxxxxxxxxxx). No id value may be null, empty, or an integer.

criteria.name: frontend_dependencies_installable
criteria.description: Running 'bun install' inside app/frontend/ completes without errors and exits with code 0. package.json lists all of the following as dependencies or devDependencies: react, react-dom, react-router-dom, react-markdown, react-syntax-highlighter, remark-gfm, vite, tailwindcss, typescript, autoprefixer, postcss, @vitejs/plugin-react. Any package from this list that is absent from package.json is a failure.

criteria.name: backend_dependencies_installable
criteria.description: Running 'pip install -r requirements.txt' inside the Python venv completes without errors and exits with code 0. requirements.txt includes all of the following packages: fastapi, uvicorn[standard], aiosqlite, httpx, python-dotenv, openai, docling-core[chunking], numpy. Any package from this list that is absent from requirements.txt is a failure.

criteria.name: fastapi_app_starts_without_errors
criteria.description: Starting uvicorn with the backend main.py produces no Python exceptions, import errors, or tracebacks in stdout/stderr during startup (after seeding completes). The process remains running and accepts HTTP connections on port 8000. Verified by issuing a GET /api/health request within 10 seconds of startup and receiving any HTTP response.
